steps.title: Use the colon as a clue
steps.content: The mongoose example comes before a colon, and the underlined phrase comes after it. That structure signals that the phrase names the broader idea illustrated by the example: same-species contact with perceived outsiders. The rest of the passage then discusses whether that kind of interaction contributes to higher cognition in animals.

steps.title: Eliminate misread functions
steps.content: Choice A treats the phrase as an example of nuanced analysis, but the later examples, such as evaluating physical status, are the analysis. Choice C says the text narrows to only a subset of species, while the passage says the dimension extends across animal species. Choice D calls the phrase a definition, but the passage introduces the idea rather than defining a scientific term.

steps.title: Name the passage focus
steps.content: The underlined phrase identifies the kind of social interaction that the whole passage examines. The author contrasts established-group behaviors with interactions involving same-species outsiders and argues that those outsider interactions may also favor sophisticated cognition. The answer is B.
